title: Settings UI Schema
description: UI schema for settings/settings.yaml form editor

forecasting.ui:title: 📈 Forecasting settings
forecasting.ui:description:
These knobs affect how DagNet estimates baseline conversion rates and how it blends forecast vs evidence when data is immature.

Because they change results, they should be committed and pulled like other shared configuration.

See Help → Forecasting settings for the full explanation and examples.

groups.title: Baseline & Blending
groups.description: Controls how DagNet estimates the baseline conversion rate from mature data and how it blends that forecast with immature evidence.

groups.title: Onset & Lag Fitting
groups.description: Controls how DagNet derives onset (the minimum conversion delay) from lag histograms and guards against pathological lognormal fits.

groups.title: Bayesian Model Priors
groups.description: Prior distributions and floors for the Bayesian compiler (Phase 2 fitting). These control the starting beliefs about conversion rates and latency parameters before data is observed.

groups.title: Convergence & Quality Gates
groups.description: Thresholds that determine when the MCMC sampler has converged and when a previous posterior is trustworthy enough to use as a warm-start.

groups.title: MCMC Sampling
groups.description: Controls the NUTS sampler configuration. Increasing draws/chains improves posterior precision at the cost of compute time.

groups.title: Cohort Maturity Fan Chart
groups.description: Controls the posterior predictive fan chart for cohort maturity analysis. The fan uses hierarchical per-cohort drift over all four parameters.

RECENCY_HALF_LIFE_DAYS.ui:help:
Half-life for mature-data recency weighting.

7–14 (responsive) · 30 (balanced) · 60–90 (conservative) · very large ≈ no recency.

LATENCY_MIN_EFFECTIVE_SAMPLE_SIZE.ui:help:
Guardrail: if recency-weighted N_eff drops below this, use a more conservative strategy.

50–150 (responsive) · 150–500 (typical) · 500+ (very conservative).

DEFAULT_T95_DAYS.ui:help:
Fallback horizon when no edge-specific t95 is available.

7–21 (typical funnels) · 30 (conservative default) · 60–90 (very conservative).

FORECAST_BLEND_LAMBDA.ui:help:
Prior strength for forecast/evidence blending.

0.05–0.20 (typical) · 0.20–0.50 (conservative) · 0 (raw evidence, no prior).

LATENCY_BLEND_COMPLETENESS_POWER.ui:help:
Extra conservatism via completeness down-weighting.

1.0 (canonical) · 1.5–3.0 (typical) · 3.0–5.0 (very conservative).

ANCHOR_DELAY_BLEND_K_CONVERSIONS.ui:help:
Credibility threshold for observed anchor-delay evidence.

20–50 (responsive) · 50–150 (typical) · 150–300 (conservative).

ONSET_MASS_FRACTION_ALPHA.ui:help:
Cumulative mass fraction defining onset day from lag histograms.

0.005 (aggressive) · 0.01 (balanced) · 0.02–0.05 (conservative).

ONSET_AGGREGATION_BETA.ui:help:
Weighted quantile for aggregating onset across slice families.

0.25 (earlier onset) · 0.5 (robust median) · 0.75 (later onset).

LATENCY_MAX_MEAN_MEDIAN_RATIO.ui:help:
Guardrail for moment-based lognormal fitting.

3–10 (enforce skew limit) · 999999 (disabled — recommended with onset shifting).

BAYES_LOG_KAPPA_MU.ui:help:
LogNormal prior centre for κ (log-space).

2.3 (κ≈10, high overdispersion) · 3.4 (κ≈30, moderate) · 4.6 (κ≈100, low).

BAYES_LOG_KAPPA_SIGMA.ui:help:
LogNormal prior width for κ.

0.5 (tight) · 1.5 (broad — default) · 3.0 (very broad).

BAYES_FALLBACK_PRIOR_ESS.ui:help:
ESS for fallback Beta prior when no warm-start posterior exists.

5–10 (weak) · 20 (moderate) · 50–100 (strong).

BAYES_DIRICHLET_CONC_FLOOR.ui:help: Minimum concentration. 0.5 = Jeffreys; 1.0 = uniform; >1 = mildly informative.

BAYES_SIGMA_FLOOR.ui:help: Below this σ, edge is treated as instant (no latency modelling).

BAYES_MU_PRIOR_SIGMA_FLOOR.ui:help: Minimum uncertainty on μ prior. Increase if fits are not converging.

BAYES_MATURITY_FLOOR.ui:help:
Observations below this completeness are excluded from κ estimation.

0.8 (inclusive) · 0.9 (balanced) · 0.95 (strict).

BAYES_SOFTPLUS_SHARPNESS.ui:help:
Onset boundary sharpness in the likelihood.

1–2 (smooth) · 5 (moderate) · 10–20 (sharp).

BAYES_RHAT_THRESHOLD.ui:help:
Max R-hat for convergence. Near 1.0 = good.

1.01 (strict) · 1.05 (standard) · 1.10 (lenient).

BAYES_ESS_THRESHOLD.ui:help:
Min ESS for convergence.

100–200 (quick fits) · 400 (standard) · 1000+ (high precision).

BAYES_WARM_START_RHAT_MAX.ui:help:
R-hat gate for warm-start acceptance. Typically looser than RHAT_THRESHOLD.

1.05 (strict) · 1.10 (standard) · 1.20 (lenient).

BAYES_DRAWS.ui:help:
Draws retained per chain. Total samples = DRAWS × CHAINS.

500 (fast) · 2000 (standard) · 5000+ (high precision).

BAYES_TUNE.ui:help:
Warmup iterations (discarded). Increase if seeing low ESS / high R-hat.

500 (fast) · 1000 (standard) · 2000+ (generous).

BAYES_CHAINS.ui:help:
Independent chains. Need ≥2 for R-hat.

2 (fast) · 4 (standard) · 8 (high reliability).

BAYES_TARGET_ACCEPT.ui:help:
NUTS acceptance rate. Higher = smaller steps, fewer divergences.

0.80 (aggressive) · 0.90 (standard) · 0.95–0.99 (conservative).

COHORT_DRIFT_FRACTION.ui:help:
Per-cohort drift as a fraction of posterior variance.

0 (off) · 0.1–0.2 (modest, default 0.20) · 0.5–1.0 (large drift, wider bands).

development.ui:title: 🛠️ Development (local)
development.ui:description: These are intended for local debugging. Avoid committing changes here unless you truly want them shared.
